title: Visual Attribute-Specific Contextual Trajectory Paradigm
description: This dataset comprises neuroimaging recordings from 37 healthy young adults performing a visual contextual trajectory paradigm. Participants viewed bound stimuli whose onset violated established trajectories in brightness, size, or orientation, with control trials presenting no violations. The study investigates attribute-specific prediction errors in visual perception and contextual processing.
methods_description: Data were collected from 37 subjects (age <35 years, normal or corrected-to-normal vision, no neurological disorders) using a visual contextual trajectory paradigm where stimulus onsets violated established trajectories in brightness, size, or orientation attributes, with control trials containing no violations.
